France.group_record: 3W-0L, 9pts, GD+8 (W3-1 SEN, W3-0 IRQ, W4-1 NOR)
France.status: Heavy favorite (76.3% straight win, only conceded 2 goals across 3 group matches)

Sweden.group_record: 1W-1L-1D, 4pts, GD-1 (W5-1 TUN, L1-5 NED, D1-1 JPN)
Sweden.status: Clear underdog (7.7% straight win) but advanced via a mixed, not-dominant group; real attacking output even in defeat (8 SOT in the 1-5 NED loss)

FRA.NOTE: France have not conceded more than 1 goal in any group match (10 scored, 2 conceded across 3 games). Fouls trend upward with opponent quality (5->8->11), consistent with Norway being the toughest test.

SWE.NOTE: Sweden's foul rate (10/12/11) and offside rate (3/3/2) are remarkably stable regardless of result (dominant win, heavy loss, draw) -- a structural team pattern, not a game-state artifact. They generated 8 SOT even in the 1-5 NED blowout loss -- genuine attacking quality, not a pushover underdog.

Kylian_Mbappe_FRA.totals: 4 goals, 2 assists in 3 matches. Hit 2+ SOT in ALL 3 group matches (4,3,2) -- perfect precedent for Q6.

Ousmane_Dembele_FRA.totals: 4 goals total, including a hat-trick against Norway in his most recent match (the freshest data point). Per Loss Pattern #6, hot streaks regress like cold streaks do -- not over-trusting this beyond what the liquid market already prices in.

Viktor_Gyokeres_SWE.totals: 1 goal in 3 matches (only vs the weakest group opponent, Tunisia). RULE15 context: Sweden are an extreme underdog (7.7% win prob) -- Scenario A (blowout underdog) suppression applies, but the liquid market is already at 19.24%, below the typical 0.20-0.35 RULE15 band -- trusting the live liquid price directly rather than stacking further suppression on top (the Enciso/GER-PAR over-suppression failure mode).

Alexander_Isak_SWE.totals: Hit 2+ SOT in 2 of 3 group matches (2,1,2) despite Sweden's underdog status. Smarkets liquid market (12.35%) is well below even a naive read of his own SOT log -- this already reflects the extreme talent/possession gap expected today. Trusting the liquid market directly per the deep-dive's PLAYER_LIQUID lesson (do not stack additional suppression on an already-liquid, already-low price).

context_flags: FRANCE_DOMINANT_FORM: 9 points from 3 wins, GD+8, only 2 goals conceded all group stage. The strongest pre-match favorite price of any knockout match so far this stage (76.3%, vs NED-MAR's 40.3% and NOR-CIV's 45.5%).

SWEDEN_REAL_BUT_OUTMATCHED: not a true minnow -- beat Tunisia 5-1, generated 8 SOT even losing 1-5 to Netherlands -- but every signal (market, Elo gap implied by the 76% favorite price, GD difference) points to a clear mismatch today specifically because France's attack (Mbappe + Dembele, 8 combined goals in 3 games) is operating at an exceptional level.

DEMBELE_HOT_STREAK_HANDLED_WITH_CAUTION: hat-trick vs Norway is the freshest data point but per Loss Pattern #6 (treating a small-sample hot/cold streak as a stable rate), not pushing the liquid anytime-scorer market (38.6%) higher on narrative alone -- the market plausibly already reflects recent form.

ISAK_GYOKERES_LIQUID_MARKETS_TRUSTED_NEAR_RAW: both are genuinely liquid (bid+offer present, narrow spreads) and already priced well below a naive RULE15 band -- per the knockout deep-dive's PLAYER_LIQUID lesson, not stacking additional underdog-suppression logic on top of an already-suppressed liquid price (the Enciso/GER-PAR over-suppression failure mode).

OFFSIDE_MODEL_DIFFERS_FROM_GER_PAR/NOR_CIV: unlike GER-PAR (clean near-zero offside record on both teams that still spiked to a disaster) or NOR-CIV (clean low record, defensively shaded up), France+Sweden's combined historical offside average is already substantial (FRA 1.33 + SWE 2.67 = 4.0/game combined), reinforced by Isak's own elevated personal rate (1,1,3) and Mbappe's recognized pace-driven offside risk. This is treated as a real signal, not a deceptively-clean record that needs aggressive upward correction -- raw Poisson P(>=4)=0.567, shaded only mildly to 0.52 for general knockout full-match-count humility.

GOAL_IN_1H_STOPPAGE_IS_AN_EARLY_WINDOW_QUESTION: per the knockout deep-dive, early-window timing questions (before the 1st hydration break) have gone 3-for-3 positive across the first 5 matches (incl. NED-MAR's identical question type, +13.38). Two independent lambda-scaling methods (from the fitted 1H-specific lambda, and from the full-match lambda) converge tightly (0.122 and 0.140) -- trusted near-raw with no late-window-style discount.

questions.text: Will the match have 3 or more total goals in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
questions.reasoning: Direct Smarkets OU2.5 goals market, Over = 0.6875. Cross-validated by independently-fit team lambdas (FRA 2.672 + SWE 0.864 = 3.536, very close to the direct market's implied total lambda of 3.501).

questions.text: Will France have 6 or more corner kicks in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
questions.reasoning: No direct 5.5 line exists; fitted lambda=6.608 from the liquid France-OU6.5-corners market (Over=0.4904), giving P(>=6)=0.6465. Cross-validated by the Most Corners market (France 74.0% to lead corners) and by the combined France+Sweden corner-lambda sum (9.995) matching the direct full-match OU8.5 fit (10.289) closely.

questions.text: Will France win in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
questions.reasoning: Direct Smarkets FTR market, France = 0.7634 (liquid, tight spread). Trimmed slightly to account for single-match variance and Sweden's real attacking output (8 SOT even in a 5-goal defeat) -- not pushed to the raw market price given France's group-stage form (3 routine wins) sets a high baseline that doesn't always hold in a single knockout fixture.

questions.text: Will Ousmane Dembélé (France) score a goal (excluding own goals) in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
questions.reasoning: Liquid anytime-goalscorer market, mid=0.3861. Dembele has 4 goals in 3 group matches including a hat-trick in his most recent match (Norway) -- a genuinely hot, current-tournament precedent, but per Loss Pattern #6 (small-sample streaks regress), not pushing materially above the market's own already-recency-aware price.

questions.text: Will France be ahead at halftime?
questions.reasoning: Direct Smarkets HT result market, France = 0.5971 (mid).

questions.text: Will Kylian Mbappé (France) have 2 or more shots on target in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
questions.reasoning: Liquid market (bid 0.6289 / offer 0.6494, mid 0.6392). Mbappe hit 2+ SOT in ALL THREE group matches (4, 3, 2) -- a perfect precedent reinforcing the already-favorable market price. Nudged slightly above raw mid per the same logic that worked for Saibari in NED-MAR (liquid market + perfect same-player precedent agreeing).

questions.text: Will a red card be shown in the match?
questions.reasoning: Direct Smarkets 'Any player to be sent off' market, mid=0.115.

questions.text: Will Viktor Gyökeres (Sweden) score a goal (excluding own goals) in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
questions.reasoning: Liquid anytime-goalscorer market, mid=0.1924. Gyokeres scored only 1 goal in 3 group matches (vs the weakest opponent, Tunisia). RULE15 (extreme-underdog forward suppression) would apply given Sweden's 7.7% win probability, but the live liquid price is already below the typical RULE15 band -- trusted directly rather than further suppressed (avoiding the Enciso/GER-PAR over-suppression failure).

questions.text: Will Alexander Isak (Sweden) have 2 or more shots on target in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
questions.reasoning: Liquid market (bid 0.1220 / offer 0.1250, mid 0.1235). Isak's own raw group-stage SOT log (2,1,2) would suggest a higher rate in isolation, but the live market already prices in today's extreme talent/possession gap against France. Trusted directly per the deep-dive's PLAYER_LIQUID lesson -- not stacking RULE15 suppression on an already-suppressed liquid price.

questions.text: Will France score the first goal of the match?
questions.reasoning: Direct Smarkets 'Team to score first goal' market, France = 0.7295 (mid).

questions.text: Will both teams score in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
questions.reasoning: Direct Smarkets BTTS market, mid=0.5715. Sweden's genuine attacking output even in their NED loss (8 SOT) supports the market's view that they're live to score even while losing.

questions.text: Will France have 7 or more shots on target in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
questions.reasoning: Direct match: question asks 7+, Smarkets has 'France over/under 6.5 shots on target' (Over = X>=7 exactly). Mid=0.5407. Consistent with France's own group-stage SOT average of 7.33.

questions.text: Will there be 5 or more total cards shown in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
questions.reasoning: Direct match: question asks 5+, Smarkets has 'Over/under 4.5 cards' (Over = X>=5 exactly). Mid=0.136.

questions.text: Will a goal be scored in first-half stoppage (added) time?
questions.reasoning: No direct stoppage-time market. Two independent lambda-scaling methods converge: (1) 1H-specific lambda (1.591, fit from 1H O/U0.5/1.5 markets) scaled to a ~4-minute stoppage window out of ~49 first-half minutes -> P(>=1)=0.122; (2) full-match lambda (3.536) scaled to 4/94 of the match -> P(>=1)=0.140. This is an EARLY-window timing question (before any hydration break) -- per the knockout deep-dive, this category has gone 3-for-3 positive across the first 5 matches, unlike late-window timing questions. Trusted near-raw.

questions.text: Will there be 4 or more offside calls in regulation (90 minutes + stoppage time)?
questions.pick_tier: TEAM_MODEL — MEDIUM CONFIDENCE, KNOCKOUT HUMILITY DISCOUNT APPLIED
questions.reasoning: No Smarkets market exists for offsides. Combined Poisson lambda from team averages: FRA 1.33 + SWE 2.67 = 4.0/game combined, raw P(>=4)=0.567. Unlike GER-PAR (a clean near-zero record that still spiked to -48.68) or NOR-CIV (clean low record, defensively shaded up), this is already a substantial, two-team-confirmed signal -- not a deceptively clean record. Reinforced by Isak's own elevated personal offside rate (1,1,3) and Mbappe's recognized pace-driven offside risk. Shaded only mildly down from the raw 0.567 to 0.52 for general full-match-count knockout-stage humility, per the standing deep-dive caution about this question family.
